source: Live session 2026-06-21: Pi generated an image via image_gen mid-conversation but could only return a Windows temp path â€” no way to deliver the file over Telegram. (Renumbered from a self-filed T-221 that collided with the profile batch; scope corrected below.)
title: Telegram rich-media SEND tool: deliver generated files (image/video/pdf/voice) mid-turn
current_state: Two delivery paths already work: inbound media (photo/video/document/voice) is received + analyzed/transcribed by the content-type handlers, and /imagine + /generate_video slash commands send a photo/video back. BUT the programmatic telegram_send TOOL (what the LLM calls during a normal turn) is TEXT-ONLY. So when Pi produces an artifact via image_gen / generate_video / a TTS or document tool in conversation, it can only return a local temp path â€” useless to Ash on a phone (observed live: 'where do I see it on laptop?').
target_state: telegram_send gains optional file delivery: telegram_send(text=..., file=<path>, kind='photo'|'video'|'document'|'audio'|'voice') routes to the matching Bot API call (send_photo/send_video/send_document/send_audio/send_voice) with the text as caption. kind defaults are inferred from file extension when omitted. After image_gen/generate_video/TTS/document tools produce a file, Pi can hand it straight to Telegram. Graceful: if no chat is configured or the send fails, fall back to returning the path + a clear note (no dead-end).
migration_plan: 1. Extend send_message / add send_file(path, kind, caption, chat_id) in tools_telegram.py wrapping bot.send_photo/send_video/send_document/send_audio/send_voice. Reuse the _chunk caption limits.

2. Extend the telegram_send ToolSpec input_schema with optional 'file' + 'kind'; infer kind from extension (.jpg/.png->photo, .mp4->video, .pdf/.pptx/.docx->document, .mp3->audio, .ogg->voice) when kind is absent.

3. Fallback: if file missing/unreadable or Telegram unreachable, return {success:false, path, note} so the caller can still tell Ash where the artifact is (resourcefulness â€” see the no-dead-ends ticket).
4. Best-effort temp cleanup after a successful send (mirror /imagine's os.unlink pattern).

5. Tests: testing/test_telegram_media_send.py (mock bot) â€” kind inferred from extension; each kind routes to the right bot method with caption; missing file returns a graceful path-fallback, not an exception.
NOTE (scope correction): inbound voice receive+transcribe and /imagine /generate_video SENDING already exist â€” do NOT rebuild them. This ticket is only the programmatic telegram_send file path.
risk_notes: Keep the tool signature backward-compatible (text-only calls must still work). Don't duplicate the existing slash-command senders â€” share the helper. Large files may exceed Telegram's 50MB bot limit; surface a clear error rather than failing silently.
